scope_note: Chapter 14 (The Theory of Wages and Unemployment) has 8 published series S1401-S1408, all drawing from two government providers via Appendix 14.2: BEA NIPA Historical Tables (T1.10 lines 1+2 for GDP and Compensation of Employees; T1.1.9 line 1 for the GDP deflator; Tables 6.5A-D for Full-Time Equivalent Employees) and BLS CPS (LNS14000000 unemployment rate; LNS13008275 mean unemployment duration). All five Phase 3 open questions are addressed in chapter_level_recommendations + phase4_open_question_resolutions blocks below. No BLS-ILC-style discontinuation impacts this chapter.

D1_source_reachability.details: REAL HEAD/GET checks performed 2026-05-18 against all 15 distinct primary/quoted/extension URLs across the 8 dossiers (see Technical/Build/_ch14_url_check_results.json + _ch14_url_check2_results.json). Tally: 13/15 returned HTTP 200 on first contact (HEAD). All six FRED extension URLs used by Ch14 — GDP, A576RC1, UNRATE, UEMPMEAN, GDPDEF, B4701C0A222NBEA — returned 200. All BEA NIPA and BLS CPS primary/quoted URLs (https://www.bea.gov/itable/national-gdp-and-personal-income; legacy http://www.bea.gov/national/index.htm#gdp; https://www.bls.gov/cps/; http://data.bls.gov/cgi-bin/surveymost?ln; http://www.bls.gov/cps/duration.htm; http://www.bls.gov/cps/cpsatabs.htm) returned 200. BEA iTable root (https://apps.bea.gov/iTable/) returned 200. Two issues: (a) S1403 extension candidate FRED W270RE1Q156NBEA (quarterly shares-of-GDI compensation) returns 404 — series ID has been retired by FRED. Substitute confirmed live: W209RC1 (Compensation of employees, paid: Domestic industries, quarterly, NIPA-consistent with Shaikh's Table 1.10 line 2) returns 200; pair with GDP (quarterly, 200) to compute A576RC1/GDP-equivalent wage share at quarterly frequency. (b) http://www.anwarshaikhecon.org/ DNS resolution failed (gaierror 11001); this is the author's personal site that hosted Appendix 14.3 data tables. Local archival copy exists at SalvagedInputs/book_data/ShaikhChoppedTables/Appendix14_InflationULdata.xlsx so loss of the live site does NOT block replication. -1 point for the W270RE1Q156NBEA 404 (substitution canonicalized, low Phase 5 cost) and noting the anwarshaikhecon.org DNS failure as offline-archival rather than blocking.

D2_year_coverage.details: Book range 1948-2011 (S1401, S1402, S1406) / 1949-2011 (S1403-S1408 due to differencing first observation loss) covered 100% by Shaikh-distributed ShaikhChoppedTables/Appendix14_InflationULdata.xlsx + Appendix14_Documentation.xlsx for level/raw observations. All six FRED extension series have data through 2024Q4 or later: GDP (1947Q1-present), A576RC1 (1948-present annual), UNRATE (1948-present monthly), UEMPMEAN (1948-present monthly), GDPDEF (1947Q1-present), B4701C0A222NBEA (1948-present annual). Post-2011 extension coverage: ~100% (2012-2024) for all 8 series. UEMPMEAN has a documented 2011 top-coding methodological break (top-coded duration raised from 2 to 5 years in January 2011) — affects S1402, S1403, S1404, S1405, S1407, S1408. The break is small in magnitude for the mean-duration calculation but must be documented and handled per the explicit Phase 4 recommendation (see Q2 resolution). -1 for the UEMPMEAN top-coding break which requires explicit continuity-adjustment decision in Phase 5/6 but does not actually withhold any years of data.

D3_license_compatibility.details: ALL sources are U.S. Government Work and therefore public-domain: BEA NIPA Historical Tables (Title 17 USC §105 — works of the federal government are not subject to copyright), BLS CPS Labor Force Statistics (same), FRED mirror of both (St. Louis Fed redistribution under public-domain terms with attribution). Local salvaged Appendix 14.3 spreadsheets are author-distributed data tables of public-domain underlying series. No commercial sources (Ibbotson/Morningstar/Conference Board) are involved in Ch14. No CC-BY attribution requirements beyond standard agency citation. Full marks.

D4_units_consistency.details: All 8 dossiers have explicit `primary_source.units` and component-level units. Documented units: GDP (billions current USD, SAAR), Compensation of Employees (billions current USD), wage share (decimal or percent), unemployment rate (percent), mean unemployment duration (weeks), duration index (1948-1951=100), unemployment intensity (decimal), GDP deflator (index — Shaikh's appendix references rebasing to '100' base implicitly, FRED's current GDPDEF vintage uses 2017=100, S1406 explicitly handles this with growth-rate splice), Full-Time Equivalent Employees (thousands of jobs), inflation (decimal annual rate), productivity (real GDP per FTE, in real USD per worker), wage-share/real-wage/nominal-wage growth (decimal annual rate). One unit consistency note that Phase 5 must enforce: Shaikh's productivity is `(GDP*100/p)/(FEE/1000)` per Appendix 14.2 p. 892 verbatim — the `*100` and `/1000` are unit-correcting factors (deflator scaled-to-100; FEE in thousands-of-jobs converted to millions) yielding productivity in real dollars per worker. The loader for S1406, S1407, S1408 MUST replicate these multipliers literally; replacing them with seemingly equivalent simplifications will silently scale the productivity series by 100,000× and break the Phillips-form regressions. -1 for this loader-time bookkeeping risk that requires explicit Phase 5 enforcement (documented below in remediation).

D5_extension_feasibility.details: All 8 series are extensible via FRED through current vintages — six FRED keys provide the entire data backbone (GDP, A576RC1, UNRATE, UEMPMEAN, GDPDEF, B4701C0A222NBEA), all confirmed HTTP 200. S1401, S1402, S1406 extend directly; S1403, S1404, S1405, S1407, S1408 extend by re-deriving (wage share, real wage, nominal wage, productivity, intensity) from the extended FRED components and re-applying HP(100) over the full extended sample (loading should re-filter, not append, to avoid endpoint bias). S1403 (quarterly phase plot) requires the substitute W209RC1 + GDP pair at quarterly frequency to replace the dead W270RE1Q156NBEA candidate (see Q1 resolution). S1405 fitted curves (1949-1982, 1994-2011 Phillips form y = a + b·x^c) need a Phase 5 decision on whether to (a) recompute the original fits, (b) refit to extended sample with original windows, (c) refit on a new window. The 1983-1993 transition gap is preserved as an analytical convention (see Q4 resolution). -1 for HP-filter endpoint bias which is intrinsic to any extension of a filtered series, mitigated by full-sample refiltering.

D6_no_proxy_compliance.details: No proxy substitutions in the data-substitution sense (the canonical agency series remain alive and reachable for all 8 series). The S1403 W270RE1Q156NBEA -> W209RC1+GDP substitution is a CANONICAL ID UPDATE within FRED (BEA NIPA mirror), not a concept-changing proxy: both fetch the same underlying NIPA Table 1.10 line 2 (Compensation of employees, paid) at quarterly frequency; the dead series was a shares-of-GDI variant that arithmetically equals the ratio of W209RC1/GDP. No Concept Match Justification block is required for this routing update, but a one-line provenance note in the S1403 loader is mandated. The CRITICAL no-proxy compliance item for this chapter is the open question on productivity definition (Q3 in chapter_level_recommendations + Q5 of Phase 3): Shaikh's productivity = real GDP per FTE, NOT BLS labor productivity per hour. Any Phase 5/6 extension MUST use the BEA-based construction (yr = (GDP*100/p)/(FEE/1000)) and explicitly REJECT BLS PRS85006092 / OPHNFB / similar labor-productivity-per-hour series as concept-inappropriate substitutes — this is documented in the chapter_level_recommendations to prevent silent proxy creep. -1 because the productivity-definition concept-policing requirement must be wired into the Phase 5 loader as a hard validation gate, not just a documentation note.

per_series.name: Nominal GDP Growth and Level of Wage Share
per_series.units: Nominal GDP in billions current USD; Compensation of employees in billions current USD; wage share in decimal (EC/GDP); nominal GDP growth in decimal annual rate
remediation: Phase 5: extend via FRED GDP (annual aggregation from quarterly SAAR) + FRED A576RC1 (annual)
Phase 5: validate annual wage-share values 1948-2011 against ShaikhChoppedTables/Appendix14_InflationULdata.xlsx cell-by-cell before splicing extension
Phase 5: freeze BEA vintage via FRED ALFRED snapshot for reproducibility (BEA periodically revises NIPA)

per_series.name: Unemployment Measures US 1948-2011
per_series.units: Unemployment rate in percent; mean duration in weeks; duration index 1948-1951=100; unemployment intensity = unemployment_rate * (duration_index/100), in decimal
proxy_flags: UEMPMEAN 2011 TOP-CODING BREAK: BLS raised the top-coded individual unemployment duration from 2 years (104 weeks) to 5 years (260 weeks) in January 2011. This affects the mean-duration calculation when the population of long-duration unemployed is non-trivial. Effect on annual mean duration is modest pre-2011 (long-spell share small) and meaningfully positive 2011-2014 (Great Recession aftermath: long-spell share ~40% of unemployed). For extension beyond 2011 the recommended treatment is: (a) USE THE POST-2011 UEMPMEAN SERIES AS-IS, with a documented annotation in the registry and visualization layer that 2011-present mean-duration values are not directly comparable in level to 1948-2010 values; (b) DO NOT apply a backward continuity adjustment (would alter Shaikh's published pre-2011 numbers); (c) For Phillips-form analysis, the duration INDEX (rebased to 1948-51) absorbs part of the break since the base-period averaging is unchanged. Mark `methodological_break_2011: true` in the registry under S1402 components.duration.
remediation: Phase 5: extend via FRED UNRATE + UEMPMEAN (monthly to annual mean aggregation)
Phase 5: confirm 1948-1951 base-window mean (4-year average of annual means of monthly observations) is computed identically to Shaikh's pre-published values
Phase 5/6: emit explicit `methodological_break: {date: 2011-01, source: BLS UEMPMEAN top-coding raised 104w -> 260w, treatment: no_adjustment_documented_break}` in registry block
Phase 6 EPR: document the 2011 break in the methodology section so downstream readers do not over-interpret post-2011 mean-duration level changes

per_series.name: Wage Share vs Unemployment Intensity (Empirical Spiral)
per_series.units: Quarterly HP(100) wage share (decimal); quarterly HP(100) unemployment intensity (decimal); time implicit
per_series.proposed_substitute: Quarterly wage-share construction: replace dead FRED W270RE1Q156NBEA (404) with W209RC1 (Compensation of employees, paid: Domestic industries, quarterly, billions USD SAAR — confirmed HTTP 200) divided by GDP (quarterly, billions USD SAAR — confirmed HTTP 200). This is a within-FRED ID update routing to the SAME underlying NIPA Table 1.10 line 2 series Shaikh used; not a concept-changing proxy. Quarterly unemployment intensity construction (Q1 RESOLUTION): aggregate monthly UNRATE and UEMPMEAN to quarterly means, rebase duration index to 1948Q1-1951Q4 mean, multiply.
proxy_flags: FRED W270RE1Q156NBEA quarterly shares-of-GDI variant is RETIRED (HTTP 404). Substitute W209RC1 + GDP at quarterly frequency yields conceptually identical wage share (EC/GDP) at quarterly frequency. Routing update; not a concept substitution.
remediation: Phase 5: update dossier extension_candidates[0].url and .source to W209RC1 + GDP (quarterly)
Phase 5: implement quarterly unemployment intensity per Q1 RESOLUTION (aggregate monthly to quarterly means, rebase duration to 1948Q1-1951Q4 base)

Phase 5: apply HP(100) at quarterly frequency (Hodrick-Prescott parameter for quarterly data conventionally = 1600, but Shaikh explicitly uses lambda=100 per Appendix 14.2 p. 893 — REPLICATE Shaikh's choice, do not substitute the textbook quarterly value)
Phase 5: validate against Appendix14_InflationULdata.xlsx quarterly tab if present, or against published Fig 14.12 phase trajectory visually

per_series.name: Rate of Change of Wage Share vs Unemployment Intensity (Raw)
per_series.units: Wage-share growth (decimal annual rate, gwsh = first-difference of natural log); unemployment intensity (decimal, ulintensity)
remediation: Phase 5: derive from extended S1401 (wage share annual) and S1402 (intensity annual)
Phase 5: confirm gwsh = ln(wage_share_t) - ln(wage_share_{t-1}) by comparing first three observations against Appendix14 published gwsh column (cell-by-cell)
Phase 5: emit unfiltered raw scatter; Fig 14.13 is the raw counterpart to Fig 14.14

per_series.name: HP(100)-Filtered Rate of Change of Wage Share vs Unemployment Intensity
per_series.units: GWSHHP100 (decimal annual rate, HP(100)-filtered); ULINTENSITYHP100 (decimal, HP(100)-filtered); Phillips form parameters a, b, c dimensionless
proxy_flags: Phillips functional form is published as y = a + x^c with implicit b=1 (Q3 RESOLUTION below). Phase 5 must fit BOTH variants (free b, constrained b=1) and report the coefficient comparison.
remediation: Phase 5: apply HP(100) to FULL extended sample (1948-2024) — do NOT append filtered values to original-window filter output (endpoint bias)

Phase 5: fit Phillips form y = a + b*x^c on (i) original windows 1949-1982 and 1994-2011, (ii) extended windows 1949-1982 and 1994-2024, (iii) optionally a third regime if post-2011 data supports it (see Q4 RESOLUTION)
Phase 5: replicate published coefficients: era 1 a=-1.026431, c=-0.010677, R²=0.931; era 2 a=-1.010996, c=-0.003709, R²=0.965; AND report free-b estimates for comparison
Phase 5: omit 1983-1993 transition window from primary fits per Shaikh's convention (Q4 RESOLUTION)

per_series.name: Inflation and Productivity Growth
per_series.units: GDP deflator (index — current FRED vintage 2017=100, growth-rate splice neutralizes base-year change); inflation (decimal annual rate, inflrate); FEE (thousands of jobs); productivity yr = (GDP*100/p)/(FEE/1000) in real USD per worker; productivity growth (decimal annual rate, GPRODVTY)
proxy_flags: PRODUCTIVITY DEFINITION POLICING (Q5 RESOLUTION): Phase 5 loader MUST construct productivity as real GDP per FTE per Shaikh's exact formula yr = (GDP*100/p)/(FEE/1000). Do NOT substitute BLS PRS85006092 (nonfarm business sector labor productivity per hour) or OPHNFB or any per-hour series. This is a HARD concept-policing requirement: Shaikh's wage share decomposition yr = wr - gwsh holds only with his per-FTE construction; using per-hour would silently break the decomposition.

GDPDEF current FRED vintage uses 2017=100 base; Shaikh's appendix is base-agnostic for growth rates and uses an unstated base for levels. Growth-rate splice is the correct extension strategy; level reconstruction at the original base is unnecessary.
remediation: Phase 5: extend via FRED GDP (annual) + GDPDEF (annual mean of quarterly) + B4701C0A222NBEA (annual FTE all industries)

Phase 5 HARD GATE: validate productivity formula literally — implement yr = (GDP*100/p)/(FEE/1000) without algebraic simplification; emit unit-test that compares output to Appendix14 published productivity column for 1948, 1972, 1996, 2011

Phase 5: chain-type vs fixed-weighted deflator question (open Q in dossier) — RESOLUTION: use chain-type GDP price index per current FRED GDPDEF, which is the BEA current standard since 1996 backward-applied. Document in registry; Shaikh's 1948-2011 sample sits entirely on the chain-type vintage as currently published.
Phase 5: FEE coverage question — RESOLUTION: use B4701C0A222NBEA which is FTE all industries (NIPA Table 6.5 line 1 broadest measure) consistent with Shaikh's economy-wide productivity intent

per_series.name: HP(100)-Filtered Rate of Change of Real Wages vs Unemployment Intensity
per_series.units: GRWAGEHP100 (decimal annual rate of real wage wr = w/p = (EC*100/FEE)/p); ULINTENSITYHP100 (decimal)
proxy_flags: Inherits productivity-definition concept-policing from S1406 (real wage construction shares the EC, FEE, p inputs).
Inherits UEMPMEAN 2011 top-coding break flag from S1402.
remediation: Phase 5: extend by re-deriving wr = (A576RC1*100/B4701C0A222NBEA)/GDPDEF, first-difference for growth rate, then HP(100) over the full extended sample

Phase 5: fit OR derive the two-era Phillips curves — open question in dossier: are the dashed curves in Fig 14.16 (a) re-fitted to real-wage growth directly, or (b) computed as S1405 wage-share-fit + HP(100)-productivity-growth? Phase 5 should emit BOTH variants and label them; (b) is the algebraically-implied curve per Eq. 14.18, (a) is the direct empirical fit. Compare visually against published Fig 14.16.
Phase 5: omit 1983-1993 transition window per Shaikh's convention

per_series.name: HP(100)-Filtered Rate of Change of Nominal Wages vs Unemployment Intensity
per_series.units: GMWAGEHP100 (decimal annual rate of nominal wage w = EC*100/FEE); ULINTENSITYHP100 (decimal)
proxy_flags: Inherits UEMPMEAN 2011 top-coding break flag from S1402.

Off-by-one window discrepancy noted in Phase 3 open questions: chapter text says Table 14.3 sample windows are 1948-1982 / 1994-2011; Appendix 14.2 Stata/EViews output shows 1949-1982 / 1994-2011. RESOLUTION: differencing loses 1948 first observation, so the regression effectively starts in 1949 regardless of how the text reports the window. Phase 5 should use 1949-1982 / 1994-2011 (the Appendix-documented windows) and note the chapter-text off-by-one in the EPR.
remediation: Phase 5: extend by re-deriving w = (A576RC1*100)/B4701C0A222NBEA, first-difference for growth rate, then HP(100) over the full extended sample
Phase 5: emit BOTH (a) direct-fit and (b) algebraic-decomposition variants of the two-era Phillips curves per S1407 remediation pattern, per Eq. 14.19: ˙w/w = ˙p/p + ˙yr/yr + f(uInt_L)

Phase 5: replicate Table 14.3 secondary regression of residual nominal-wage growth on inflation + productivity growth; published coefficient on inflation in era 1 ≈ slightly below 1, on productivity growth ≈ 0.82
Phase 5: use sample windows 1949-1982 / 1994-2011 per Appendix 14.2

chapter_level_recommendations: Q1 RESOLUTION (Phase 3 OQ #1, S1403 quarterly intensity construction): Shaikh's footnote 10 (p. 666) states 'Unemployment intensity was not available at the quarterly level'. RECOMMENDED CANONICAL CONSTRUCTION for the quarterly Fig 14.12 phase plot: (i) aggregate monthly UNRATE to quarterly means; (ii) aggregate monthly UEMPMEAN to quarterly means; (iii) rebase duration index to the 1948Q1-1951Q4 average (16 quarters) instead of the annual 1948-1951 base; (iv) intensity = quarterly_UNRATE * (quarterly_duration_index / 100). This produces a true quarterly intensity (the literal reading of footnote 10 is that BLS did not publish a pre-aggregated quarterly intensity, NOT that monthly data was unavailable). Alternative reading — Shaikh annualized intensity then forward-filled to quarters — produces a step-function intensity that visibly contradicts the Fig 14.12 phase trajectory's intra-year curvature. Use construction (i)-(iv).

Q2 RESOLUTION (Phase 3 OQ #2, UEMPMEAN 2011 top-coding break): DO NOT apply a continuity adjustment. Use raw FRED UEMPMEAN throughout the extended sample. Annotate the registry with `methodological_break_2011-01: true` under S1402.components.duration and surface this in the EPR. Rationale: (i) backward adjustment would alter Shaikh's published pre-2011 figures; (ii) the duration INDEX (rebased to 1948-51) absorbs much of the level break since the base is unchanged; (iii) the intensity Phillips-curve relationships are estimated on HP(100)-filtered data which smooths abrupt level shifts; (iv) BLS did not publish a back-revised series, so any adjustment would be analyst-estimated. Document the break, do not adjust.

Q3 RESOLUTION (Phase 3 OQ #3, S1405 Phillips b coefficient): Appendix 14.2 reports the fit as y = C(1) + x^C(3), equivalent to constraining b=1 in y = a + b*x^c. Phase 5 should: (i) replicate the published constrained fit (b=1) and verify the reported coefficients a=-1.026431, c=-0.010677 (era 1) and a=-1.010996, c=-0.003709 (era 2); (ii) ALSO estimate the unconstrained y = a + b*x^c and report b along with a, c; (iii) document both in the EPR. The constrained form is Shaikh's published choice; the unconstrained estimate is the more general parameterization and serves as a robustness check.

Q4 RESOLUTION (Phase 3 OQ #4, 1983-1993 transition window): PRESERVE the omission in Phase 5 fits to maintain comparability with Shaikh's published results. For Phase 6 extension analysis, evaluate whether 2008-2014 (Global Financial Crisis aftermath) constitutes a THIRD regime shift by: (i) fitting the second era curve on 1994-2007 vs 1994-2024 and comparing parameter stability; (ii) if a third regime emerges, document it with the same convention — fit two stable windows, omit the transition. Do NOT attempt to interpolate or fit across transition windows.

Q5 RESOLUTION (Phase 3 OQ #5, S1406 productivity definition): HARD CONCEPT-POLICING RULE for Phase 5 loader. Productivity = real GDP per FTE per Shaikh's exact formula yr = (GDP*100/p)/(FEE/1000), using BEA NIPA inputs (GDP from T1.10/T1.1.5, p from T1.1.9, FEE from T6.5A-D). REJECT and FAIL the loader if any per-hour series (BLS PRS85006092, OPHNFB, OPHPBS, OPHMFG) is substituted — these measure a different concept (output per hour) and would silently invalidate Shaikh's wage-share decomposition. Emit `concept_policing_assertion: productivity_per_FTE_not_per_hour` in the registry under S1406 and inherited by S1407, S1408.
S1403 URL UPDATE: replace dead FRED W270RE1Q156NBEA (404) extension candidate with W209RC1 + GDP at quarterly frequency. Within-FRED routing update; not a concept-changing substitution.

OFFLINE-ARCHIVAL: http://www.anwarshaikhecon.org/ DNS resolution fails (gaierror). The author's site that hosted Appendix 14.3 data tables is offline. Local archival copy exists at Inputs/Capitalism Data/SalvagedInputs/book_data/ShaikhChoppedTables/Appendix14_InflationULdata.xlsx and Appendix14_Documentation.xlsx — use these as the canonical published-values reference for Phase 5 cell-by-cell validation.

PHASE 5 CELL-BY-CELL VALIDATION GATE: before any extension splice, every Phase 5 loader for Ch14 series MUST validate the original-window reconstruction against ShaikhChoppedTables/Appendix14_InflationULdata.xlsx — this is the single biggest replication-faithfulness check for the chapter and the primary defense against silent loader bugs in the unit-scaling-sensitive productivity formula (Q5).

HP FILTER PARAMETER REPLICATION: Shaikh explicitly uses lambda=100 for ALL HP filtering in Ch14 including quarterly Fig 14.12 (Appendix 14.2 p. 893: 'filtered by the Hodrick-Prescott (HP) filter with the default parameter of 100'). The textbook quarterly value is 1600; DO NOT substitute. Replicate lambda=100.
Add `adequacy.status: ready_for_phase5` to all 8 series in series_registry.json (per registry_delta).
open_questions: Q1 RESOLVED: S1403 quarterly intensity construction — aggregate monthly UNRATE/UEMPMEAN to quarterly means, rebase to 1948Q1-1951Q4 base.
Q2 RESOLVED: UEMPMEAN 2011 top-coding break — do not adjust; document and annotate.
Q3 RESOLVED: S1405 Phillips b coefficient — published fit uses b=1 constrained; Phase 5 emits both constrained and unconstrained variants.
Q4 RESOLVED: 1983-1993 transition window — preserve omission; evaluate third regime in Phase 6 extension.
Q5 RESOLVED: Productivity = real GDP per FTE per BEA (not per-hour per BLS); concept-policing assertion enforced by loader.

phase4_open_question_resolutions.ch14_phase3_q1_S1403_quarterly_intensity: Aggregate monthly UNRATE and UEMPMEAN to quarterly means; rebase duration index to 1948Q1-1951Q4 (16-quarter) base; intensity = quarterly_UNRATE * (quarterly_duration_index/100). Footnote 10 indicates BLS did not publish a pre-aggregated quarterly intensity, not that monthly inputs were unavailable.
phase4_open_question_resolutions.ch14_phase3_q2_UEMPMEAN_2011_topcoding_b: Use raw FRED UEMPMEAN throughout extended sample. Annotate `methodological_break_2011-01: true` in registry. Rationale: backward adjustment alters Shaikh's published numbers; duration INDEX rebase to 1948-51 absorbs part of break; HP(100) smooths abrupt level shifts.
phase4_open_question_resolutions.ch14_phase3_q3_S1405_phillips_b_coeffici: Published fit uses b=1 constrained form y = C(1) + x^C(3). Phase 5 emits BOTH constrained (replicating a=-1.026431, c=-0.010677 era1; a=-1.010996, c=-0.003709 era2) and unconstrained y = a + b*x^c (reporting all three parameters) variants.
phase4_open_question_resolutions.ch14_phase3_q4_1983_1993_transition_wind: Preserve omission for primary fits. For Phase 6 extension, evaluate post-2007 regime stability by fitting era2 on 1994-2007 vs 1994-2024 and comparing parameter stability; if a third regime emerges, document with same convention (fit two stable windows, omit transition).
phase4_open_question_resolutions.ch14_phase3_q5_S1406_productivity_defini: HARD CONCEPT-POLICING RULE: productivity = real GDP per FTE per Shaikh's exact formula yr = (GDP*100/p)/(FEE/1000) using BEA NIPA inputs. REJECT per-hour substitutes (PRS85006092, OPHNFB, OPHPBS, OPHMFG). Inherited by S1407, S1408 via the wage share decomposition.

url_reachability_evidence.method: urllib HEAD with GET fallback for 403/405/501/timeout, UA=RSCD-P4-AdequacyBot/1.0, timeout=12s

substitutions_recorded.rationale: W270RE1Q156NBEA returns HTTP 404 (FRED retired). W209RC1 (Compensation of employees, paid: Domestic industries, quarterly) and GDP (quarterly) jointly reconstruct the quarterly wage share Shaikh used. Both 200. Within-FRED routing update; not a concept substitution.

offline_archival_notes.treatment: offline-archival; local copy at Inputs/Capitalism Data/SalvagedInputs/book_data/ShaikhChoppedTables/Appendix14_InflationULdata.xlsx and Appendix14_Documentation.xlsx serves as canonical published-values reference

url_reachability_evidence.auth_required_session_notes: FRED bulk access requires free API key (FRED_API_KEY env var); HEAD/landing pages are public. BEA iTable bulk download requires no auth but is interactive (recommend BEA API instead for Phase 5 — free registration). BLS CPS public bulk download via /timeseries/data/?seriesid=LNS14000000 (no auth). None blocking for Phase 4 adequacy.

productivity_concept_policing_block.context: Phase 3 OQ #5 surfaced the risk that future loaders/analysts could silently substitute BLS per-hour labor productivity (OPHNFB, PRS85006092) for Shaikh's per-FTE construction. This block ratifies the concept-policing rule that Phase 5 loaders MUST enforce.
productivity_concept_policing_block.shaikh_definition_verbatim: yr = productivity = (GDP*100/p)/(FEE/1000) [Appendix 14.2 p. 892 verbatim, where GDP from NIPA T1.10 line 1, p = GDP deflator from NIPA T1.1.9 line 1, FEE = full-time equivalent employees from NIPA T6.5A-D]
productivity_concept_policing_block.concept: Real GDP per full-time-equivalent worker (annual, economy-wide)
must_reject_substitutes: BLS OPHNFB (output per hour, nonfarm business sector)
BLS PRS85006092 (nonfarm business sector labor productivity index)
BLS OPHPBS (output per hour, business sector)
productivity_concept_policing_block.rationale: Per-hour productivity differs from per-FTE productivity by the hours-per-worker trend (declining secularly). Substituting per-hour silently breaks Shaikh's wage-share decomposition wr = w/p, gwsh = wr - yr (Eq. 14.18-14.19), because both wr and yr use the same FEE denominator in his construction.
productivity_concept_policing_block.phase5_implementation: Loader for S1406, S1407, S1408 emits assertion failure if any per-hour series is detected in extension_candidates. Registry block under S1406 includes `concept_policing_assertion: productivity_per_FTE_not_per_hour`.

uempmean_2011_break_block.context: BLS raised the top-coded individual unemployment duration from 2 years (104 weeks) to 5 years (260 weeks) in January 2011. This affects the mean-duration computation when long-spell unemployed are a material share of the unemployed.
uempmean_2011_break_block.magnitude_assessment: Pre-2011 effect is small (long-spell share of unemployed historically <15%, top-coding affected few observations). Post-2011 effect is meaningful 2011-2014 because the Great Recession produced a large long-spell cohort (~40% of unemployed at peak in 2010), so the unbiased 2011+ series shows higher mean duration than a counterfactual 2-year-top-coded continuation would have.
rationale: Backward adjustment would alter Shaikh's published 1948-2011 figures, defeating the purpose of replication.
The duration INDEX (rebased to 1948-51 average) absorbs part of the level break: rebasing divides by an unchanged base-period mean.
HP(100) filtering smooths abrupt one-period level shifts.
BLS did not publish a back-revised consistent series; any adjustment would be analyst-estimated.

registry_annotation.S1402.components.duration: methodological_break_2011-01: {description: 'BLS raised UEMPMEAN top-coding from 104w to 260w', treatment: 'no_adjustment_documented_break', affects_extension: true}

hp_filter_lambda_replication_block.context: Shaikh uses HP filter lambda=100 throughout Ch14, including the quarterly Fig 14.12. The textbook convention is lambda=100 for annual data and lambda=1600 for quarterly. Shaikh's explicit choice deviates from the quarterly convention.
hp_filter_lambda_replication_block.shaikh_choice_verbatim: Rates of change of w, wr, σW as well as the unemployment rate and intensity were filtered by the Hodrick–Prescott (HP) filter with the default parameter of 100. [Appendix 14.2 p. 893]
hp_filter_lambda_replication_block.phase5_rule: Use lambda=100 for ALL HP filtering in Ch14 including quarterly S1403. Do NOT substitute lambda=1600 for quarterly. Document Shaikh's choice in EPR.
hp_filter_lambda_replication_block.consequence: Lambda=100 on quarterly data produces less smoothing than lambda=1600; preserves the 'zigs and zags' Shaikh references in footnote 10.
